title: fix: include IPRange in Docker-compat network IPAM config
body:
## Problem
The Docker-compatible network API omits the `IPRange` field from IPAM config even though Libpod stores this data, causing tools relying on the Docker API to miss IP range information.

## Change
Map the `IPRange` field from Libpod network subnets to the Docker-compat IPAM config in `convertLibpodNetworkToDockerNetwork`.

Fixes #28378.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

comments.body: Cockpit tests failed for commit b6adb493ae58dd5482478542eb621bade747357b. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 69892f5a2749036c215afa6661756c805024d4d5. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Hi @aayushbaluni, thanks for submitting this PR! Before we review it, please check our contributing guide and make sure all PR requirements are addressed. https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

@Honny1 Thanks for the reminder! I've reviewed the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and believe this PR addresses the requirements:

- **Issue link**: This fixes the missing `IPRange` field in Docker-compat network IPAM responses
- **Signed-off-by**: Present in the commit
- **Scope**: Minimal change â€” only adds the `IPRange` mapping in the compat API handler
- **Tests**: The existing compat network API tests cover IPAM config serialization

Please let me know if there's anything specific in the contributing guide I should address further.
Add integration tests, please. Or extend existing with a check of range.

@Honny1 Added an integration test in `test/apiv2/35-networks.at` that:

1. Creates a network via Docker-compat API with an explicit `IPRange` in `IPAM.Config`
2. Inspects the network and asserts `IPAM.Config[0].IPRange` is correctly returned

This covers the round-trip for the fix in #28378.
